Good news only, since 2026 Hand-picked from across the web Verified before it cheers you up

Kindness Animals Everyday Heroes Science Wins Good AI The Planet Community Comebacks Reunions The Big Picture
The Big Picture

Things are better than the headlines suggest.

It rarely makes the front page, because progress is slow and bad news is loud. But look at it decade by decade and the trend is unmistakable: across the long arc, humanity is getting healthier, safer, more literate and less poor. Here's the proof — in the numbers.

Extreme poverty

▼ 78% since 1990
8.5% from 38% in 1990

Share of the world living on less than ~$2.15/day. It has fallen by more than three-quarters in a single generation.

Source: World Bank / Our World in Data

Child mortality

▼ 80% since 1960
3.7% from 18.3% in 1960

Share of children who die before their fifth birthday. It has fallen in every decade since 1960 — from nearly 1 in 5 to fewer than 1 in 25.

Source: UN IGME / World Bank

Child deaths per year

▼ 62% since 1990
4.9M from 12.8M in 1990

The number of children under five who die each year, most from preventable causes — down by well over half since 1990.

Source: UN IGME / UNICEF

Maternal mortality

▼ 42% since 1990
223 from 385 in 1990 · per 100k births

Mothers who die from pregnancy or childbirth, per 100,000 births. Down by more than 40% since 1990 as care has reached more women.

Source: WHO / UN MMEIG

Life expectancy

▲ 1.6× since 1950
73.2 yrs from 46.5 yrs in 1950

How long a newborn can expect to live, worldwide. It has risen in nearly every decade since 1950.

Source: UN WPP / Our World in Data

Childhood immunization

▲ +63 pts since 1980
83% from 20% in 1980

Share of infants getting the standard three-dose DTP vaccine. From just 1 in 5 in 1980 to more than 4 in 5 today.

Source: WHO / UNICEF

Adult literacy

▲ +11 pts since 1990
87% from 76% in 1990

Share of adults who can read and write. Nearly nine in ten people can today — and the share keeps climbing.

Source: UNESCO / Our World in Data

Access to electricity

▲ +20 pts since 1990
91% from 71% in 1990

Share of the world with electricity at home. More than two billion people have gained access since 1990.

Source: World Bank / Our World in Data

Homicide in Western Europe

▼ 98% since 1300
1 from 41 in 1300 · per 100k people

Murders per 100,000 people — the very long view. Everyday lethal violence has collapsed to a tiny fraction of medieval levels.

Source: Our World in Data (Eisner)

How Cloudbreak works

We comb publishers across the web for the stories that make your day a little brighter, show you the headline and a quick summary, then send you straight to the original source for the full story. We don't republish anyone's work — good news belongs to everyone, and credit belongs to the people who reported it.

Spotted something good?

A neighbor, a rescue, a small win on your street — if it made you smile, send it our way.

Send us a tip →
